title: Active EMS Support in Vehicular Accidents
summary: By 2015, Asingan Fire Station under FINSP LORETO M BERNARDINO was already actively helping in vehicular accident response. At the time, BFP personnel with nursing background worked alongside the LGU ambulance in emergency response, and EMS supplies were supported by the LGU.
sourceLabel: Project oral history from station account
sourceStatus: Working local record saved on May 17, 2026.
notes: The exact start date within 2015 has not yet been confirmed.
The current account emphasizes EMS activity even before the later transport assets became more prominent.
Based on the current station account, EMS supplies were fully supported by the LGU.

title: L300 Period and Increased Recognition in Patient Transport
summary: When the L300 was turned over to the station, BFP Asingan became more recognized for transporting patients, strengthening its EMS presence in the community.
sourceLabel: Project oral history from station account
sourceStatus: Working local record saved on May 17, 2026.
notes: The vehicle is currently saved with a 2017 turnover year, but the exact turnover date is still unconfirmed.

title: Toyota Japan Ambulance and Sustained High EMS Volume
summary: After the arrival of the Toyota Japan ambulance, Asingan Fire Station's EMS activity intensified, with annual EMS responses reportedly staying close to or not below 100 per year.
sourceLabel: Project oral history from station account
sourceStatus: Working local record saved on May 17, 2026.
notes: The exact arrival date of the Toyota Japan ambulance is not yet confirmed in the current project records.
The annual response figure is currently saved as local station account rather than as a formal statistical report.
